Greenfield Farmers Market

Welcome to Greenfield Farmers Market, a community gathering place where local growers and artisan producers bring their finest goods every Saturday morning. We have been serving the Greenfield community for over twenty years, connecting families with the people who grow and make their food.

Market Hours

We are open every Saturday from 8am to 1pm, running from April through November. During the peak summer months of July and August we also open on Wednesday evenings from 4pm to 7pm. The market takes place rain or shine in the Greenfield Town Square.

Our Vendors

Greenfield Farmers Market is home to more than forty regular vendors. You will find certified organic vegetable farms, heritage breed meat producers, dairy farms offering raw and pasteurised milk and cheese, orchards selling fresh and preserved fruit, honey producers, fresh-cut flower growers, and a rotating selection of bakers and prepared food stalls.

Produce

Sunrise Valley Farm brings seasonal vegetables including heirloom tomatoes, sweet corn, summer squash, kale, chard, and a wide variety of salad greens. Cedar Ridge Organics specialises in root vegetables and winter squash. Maple Hill Farm grows strawberries, raspberries, and blueberries throughout the summer season.

Meat and Dairy

Rolling Meadows Ranch offers pasture-raised beef, pork, and lamb. All animals are raised without antibiotics or added hormones on open pasture. Blue Sky Dairy sells raw milk, aged cheddar, fresh chèvre, and seasonal yoghurt. Their herd of mixed-breed goats and Jersey cows graze on certified organic pasture year-round.

Bakery and Preserves

Heartland Bakehouse bakes sourdough loaves, rye bread, cinnamon rolls, and seasonal fruit tarts using locally milled heritage grain flours. Grandma Rosa's Kitchen offers small-batch jams, pickles, relishes, and fermented vegetables made from produce sourced directly from fellow market vendors.

Getting Here

The market is located at 14 Town Square, Greenfield. Ample free parking is available in the municipal car park on Oak Street, one block from the market entrance. The number 7 bus stops directly in front of the Town Square on its route from the train station.

Community Programs

We accept SNAP and EBT payments at the market information booth. Participants receive a dollar-for-dollar match through our Fresh Bucks programme, up to twenty dollars per visit. We also run a free weekly cooking demonstration at 10am every Saturday, led by local chefs and nutritionists.

Contact

For vendor enquiries, sponsorship opportunities, or general information please email info@greenfieldmarket.example or call 555-0142 during office hours, Monday to Friday 9am to 5pm.
